description: Public-safe durable checkpoint and human-interrupt conformance fixture. Defines checkpoint states, interrupt scenarios, pause/resume transitions, replay safety assertions, audit trace export schema, and artifact version lineage. Frozen at Contract v0 (2026-05-09). No production DB mutation implied.

v0Freeze.note: No new checkpoint states, interrupt decision types, or replay guarantees may be added without a v0→v1 compatibility plan.

paused.description: Task execution safely suspended at a checkpoint. Worker released.

awaiting_operator.description: Task cannot proceed without operator input (human interrupt).

interruptDecisionTypes.description: Operator must confirm a safety-sensitive operation is acceptable.
Task instructions are unclear or contradictory.
Task requires permission the worker cannot self-grant.
Worker detected a conflict (branch, lock, concurrent task).

scenarios.name: worker-checkpoints-during-safe-file-operation
scenarios.description: Worker reaches a natural pause point after completing a file write and checkpoints before proceeding.

when.reason: safe pause after core contract write and validation

scenarios.name: worker-resumes-from-checkpoint
scenarios.description: Operator or automation triggers resume; worker reclaims task and continues.

scenarios.name: pause-timeout-transitions-to-cancelled
scenarios.description: A paused task that exceeds the pause timeout is cancelled by the broker.

scenarios.name: human-interrupt-safety-gate-approved
scenarios.description: Worker encounters a safety gate, operator approves, task continues.

given.riskSummary: Redacted: proposed change touches safety-sensitive contract area

when.summary: Redacted summary of safety-sensitive operation for operator review

operatorAction.comment: Redacted operator comment confirming safety acceptance

scenarios.name: human-interrupt-ambiguous-scope-clarified
scenarios.description: Worker cannot interpret task; operator clarifies scope, task continues.

given.ambiguity: Task asks for 'contract update' without specifying which contract or what change

when.summary: Redacted summary of scope ambiguity presented to operator

operatorAction.comment: Redacted operator clarification of intended scope

scenarios.name: human-interrupt-refused-becomes-block
scenarios.description: Operator refuses the interrupt; task transitions to blocked terminal state.

given.summary: Redacted summary of required permission request

then.reason: Operator refused approval for requested operation.

scenarios.name: human-interrupt-conflict-detected-operator-chooses-resolution
scenarios.description: Worker detects a branch conflict; operator chooses to proceed with force option.

given.conflict: Target branch has diverged; worker cannot safely merge without direction

when.summary: Redacted summary of detected conflict and resolution options presented to operator

operatorAction.comment: Redacted operator decision on conflict resolution path

scenarios.name: interrupt-timeout-transitions-to-cancelled
scenarios.description: An awaiting_operator task that exceeds the interrupt timeout is cancelled.

scenarios.name: replay-completed-task-is-idempotent-noop
scenarios.description: Replaying a completed task returns the terminal state without side effects.

scenarios.name: replay-checkpoint-resume-does-not-re-execute-pre-checkpoint-ops
scenarios.description: Resuming from a checkpoint must not replay already-completed pre-checkpoint operations.

auditTraceExport.description: Redacted deterministic JSON event sequence for a task's full lifecycle including checkpoint and interrupt events.

artifactVersionLineage.description: Example redacted artifact version lineage for checkpoint-interrupt contract v0.

assertions: paused and awaiting_operator are non-terminal transitory states
checkpoint records are redacted: no secrets, private endpoints, provider identifiers, runtime dumps, or host-specific paths
resume from checkpoint does not replay completed pre-checkpoint operations
human interrupt requires operator.decide before task can proceed to running or blocked
refused interrupt produces blocked terminal evidence satisfying the Block contract
replay of terminal task produces no new side effects
audit trace export is deterministic, redacted, and contains no provider message ids above accepted-send
artifact version lineage uses repo-relative paths and deterministic version refs only
all scenarios confirm terminalOutboxAckMutated: false and liveProviderSend: false
checkpoint and interrupt contracts do not imply production DB mutation or persistence rollout
all v0 assertions are consistent with existing task-lifecycle, cancellation-idempotency, and terminal-semantics contracts
